title: tephroseris integrifolia subsp. aurantiaca
intro: Tephroseris integrifolia subsp. aurantiaca, also known as Golden Geranium or Mountain Everlasting, is a flowering plant species native to the mountainous regions of the Himalayas and the Tibetan Plateau. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and exhibiting antioxidant properties, making it a potential natural remedy for various health concerns. Golden Geranium is traditionally prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and is often used in Ayurvedic and traditional Chinese medicine practices.
